title: Denied Exploit Attempts at OT DMZ Boundary

spl:
index=ot_sec earliest=-24h (sourcetype="pan:threat" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts" OR sourcetype="claroty:alert")
| eval severity=coalesce(severity, priority, level)
| where action="blocked" OR disposition="blocked" OR threat_id!="none"
| stats count by signature, src_ip, dest_zone, sourcetype
| sort - count
description: Aggregates blocked threats at OT firewall and IDS boundaries to prove active zone boundary protection.
value: Aggregates blocked threats at OT firewall and IDS boundaries to prove active zone boundary protection.

grandmaExplanation: Aggregates blocked threats at OT firewall and boundaries to prove active zone boundary protection ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), ES (optional).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:threat`, `nozomi:alerts`, `claroty:alert`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Normalize severity scales; (2) enrich with MITRE if desired; (3) tune noisy signatures; (4) daily SOC rollup for management.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sec earliest=-24h (sourcetype="pan:threat" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts" OR sourcetype="claroty:alert")
| eval severity=coalesce(severity, priority, level)
| where action="blocked" OR disposition="blocked" OR threat_id!="none"
| stats count by signature, src_ip, dest_zone, sourcetype
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Denied Exploit Attempts at OT DMZ Boundary (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.2)** ‚Äî Aggregates blocked threats at OT firewall and IDS boundaries to prove active zone boundary protection.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:threat`, `nozomi:alerts`, `claroty:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), ES (optional). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sec; **sourcetype**: pan:threat, nozomi:alerts, claroty:alert. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot_sec, sourcetype="pan:threat", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **severity** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where action="blocked" OR disposition="blocked" OR threat_id!="none"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by signature, src_ip, dest_zone, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Denied Exploit Attempts at OT DMZ Boundary (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.2)** ‚Äî Aggregates blocked threats at OT firewall and IDS boundaries to prove active zone boundary protection.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:threat`, `nozomi:alerts`, `claroty:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), ES (optional). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar (top signatures), Map (src geo), Table.
